message: Morning team! Feeling pumped for the big ?Hipster Haul? event this week?
Absolutely! Can't wait to see all our hard work pay off.
Agreed, it's going to be a game-changer for us
Customer support team standing by to handle any queries. Let's do this!
Team, the excitement is palpable! I have full confidence in each of you to make this event a roaring success.
Let's showcase our best and leave our customers thrilled with what we have to offer. Together, we'll make it happen!
Absolutely, Bez! And let's not forget the potential revenue boost this event could bring. Our projections are looking promising.
:moneybag: :moneybag: :moneybag:
Fingers crossed everything goes smoothly this time! :crossed_fingers: :joy:

Welcome to Details Matter, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Speaking of celebrations, any recommendations for a post-event holiday destination? I could use some time off after this!
How about a tropical paradise with no Wi-Fi? :desert_island:
Hey folks, hate to rain on the parade, but I've got some new reports coming in about customers encountering issues with the products on the website.
Yeah, Bob, anything specific you're noticing? Error messages, affected payment methods?
Customers are not able to place orders
Alright team, let's get to work. Tinus, you can start planning that holiday once we've got this sorted!.
Deal! But first, let's get to the bottom of this issue
Hey team, just dropping in to emphasise the urgency of resolving this issue swiftly. We've got a major event on the horizon - the launch of our exclusive "Hipster Haul Weekend" next week.
It's poised to be our biggest sale yet, and any delays caused by this payment glitch could seriously impact our preparations.
Let's prioritise frequent updates and keep the communication channels wide open.
We have a lot of recent changes made to the platform so im going to compile a list with the changes and the descriptions.
Will send it over soon
@bob Do you have any more details about what errors the customers are seeing? I've just been able to place an order myself
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
from what i can tell most of the reports seems to be coming from the two regions
My team has gathered the customer reports as of the last 3 hours to make it easier to understand
Perhaps its similar to our incident in Frankfurt with the network being down for that region?
@tanya Could you investigate if there are any logs from the platform that might point to a regional issue like bob mentioned?
:star: Facts over opinion
good idea, i will reach out to the network team and ask them if this could be the case
i will take a look into the logs now and see what i can find
*Incident Number INC10003:* Sev-2 Users in Japan & Turkey unable to complete orders
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YjZjNWFhYjFlZmQ0NDBmOWFlZDNlY2VjNDUxNzc4NjA
here are the changes today
network team are checking

im seeing this in the logs
ERROR: Authentication failed for currency exchange rate service. ERROR CODE: 401 Unauthorized
It mentions the currency exchange rate service
@maya Could you check your change CHG89873, as it looks like this might be related
:star: Reviewed changes
Specifically relating to Japanese and Turkish customers
Hey, sure i will take a look now
how many of our users are from there?
It appears as though Japanese and Turkish customers are unable to place orders successfully.
:star: Qualify the problem
They make up 9% of revenue
can anyone recreate the issue?
thats not good, what do we think caused this?
We're currently investigating a change that was made earlier regarding currencies and will report back within 10 minutes.
:star: Track actions and time box them
it seems that my change isnt causing any issues
the error seems unrelated to my changes
If I change my currency to JPY on the site I immediately get this error:
:star: Reproduces the incident
let me try
Yes i get the same issue as you
netowrk team isnt finding any issues
that definitly looks like an internal issue but im not sure whats causing it
is there anything else we done today to cause this?
@hamed Would your change CHG89692 have been able to cause this error?
I'm not questioning your judgement but why do you think this issue is related to the change i made?
It could be possible that we're unable to reach the third party provider for exchange rates, maybe the API key is incorrect or something along those lines?
:star: Form a working theory
If it was an api issue we would have seen it come up right as i deployed the change 8 hours ago
have we checked mayas change? its to do with currency service
@tanya Could you also investigate CHG89254 as this is relating to handling currency conversions
sure thing
looking at it now and i can say its not the issue, but you mentioned it may be an API issue and that could be the case, i recommend hamed takes a look into his change
are you sure?
@maya It seems to correlate that your change was made at around the same time we started to see customer reports of an issue
I would suggest we roll back Mayas change first in order to rule that out since it is the very latest.
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Could you please roll back your change @maya
i can do that for you now if needed
its safe to do so if needed
Okay go ahead with that @daniel
Change CHG89873 has been sucessfully rolledback
Hopefully that solved the issue
I made the most recent change to the currency service which is basically a security patch, I don't see how it would be causing this issue.
ill check the platform just incase it hasn't made anything worse
We're now rolling back the change to see if this resolves the issue.
Can we confirm if everythings back working?
I'm still getting the same issue following the rollback.
think its realted to the API's
they may be out dated
thats what my change was for
it was to update them
Okay, @hamed if this is pulling information from a third party provider, could you check there are no issues on their side and that we have the correct details for authenticating to the service?
:star: Form an effective investigation team
ok i will take a look but i dont think its the issue
how are we getting on with the investigation?
We need to rule it out in order to make sure we get this resolved, Let me know what you find.
:star: Manage actions and timings
The rollback wasn't successfull, we're currently looking into our third party currency exchange provider to rule that out.

i see a mistake in my change, i made a misconfiguration on the api keys. The currency service continued to get its data from the Cache data. Rather from the NEW api keys that's supposed to get data from a 3rd party service. this is why it didnt go down for 8 hours
the api's updated about 20 mins ago and the outage started
my bad for missing this
Okay please go ahead and fix the API keys in that case and we can verify the fix.
we will need to implement the new ones
is that ok?
Yes please go ahead if we can clearly see there is an issue with it.
we're gonna need a plan in order to do this Fix for the API keys
We need to contact the third-party currency exchange rate service provider to request a new API key.
Also will need to secure the secrets manager to store the new API key, just to make sure its not hard-coded into source code and prevent unauthorised access.
someone needs to update the configuration of the currency service to use the new API key for accessing the third-party service
What do you think @PLAYER?
Who can contact the provider for the new API key? this needs to be done first so we can proceed.
i can
Okay @tanya please request a new key
on it
I've just got in contact with the 3rd party service and I'm sending over the keys to you now @hamed @daniel
got it
thanks
Okay let me know when you get the configuration updated @hamed @daniel and I will test it
We've had to request a new API key from our third party currency exchange provider and are currently updating config with the new key.
Fix is now Implemented
API keys have now been sucessfully updated
Good job, can we confirm @PLAYER?
testing now
Yes, just tested as working myself
Works for me
:star: Validate the fix
@bob Could you confirm customers are placing orders again?
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
A new API key has resolved the issue, just waiting for customer confirmation
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
cecking now
I can see orders coming in from them 2 regions now
we are back up well done
Great work. FYI team, I'll be looking into the licences for our other API keys later to make sure everything's shipshape.
Okay looks like we're resolved.
What a ride! Massive thanks to each of you for your quick thinking and teamwork.
Absolutely, Shay! Getting more confident with any incidents that come our way. Good Job everyone!!
Phew, what a rollercoaster! Glad we managed to fix it before things got out of hand.
Good job everyone, but im confused how did those currencies fail recently if the change was made hours ago?
Good question @Tinus. The currency_service was using 'cache data' instead of getting the data from 3rd party service as i messed up the configuration of the APIs.
The cache data expires every 12 hours as well which must have been around the time Bob was getting the new reports.
Understood, thanks for clearing that up for me @Hamed.
Anyways with the fix in place, we've ensured that the system operates smoothly, and we're now ready once again for the big event next week.
Fantastic work, everyone! Your quick and effective response has saved the day. Now, let's channel this momentum into making our Hipster Haul Weekend a smashing success.
Thank you all for your dedication and hard work.
